mappings.notes: Adversary introduces malicious data into training pipeline — corrupts model behaviour in ways baked into weights
Hidden functionality embedded in model weights — triggered by specific inputs, invisible to standard testing
Adversarially crafted training examples designed to produce specific model behaviours without detection
Data and model poisoning categorised as Critical — specific attack vectors mapped per training pipeline
Adversarial testing covering poisoning detection in training pipelines before each production promotion
Data quality measurement and validation applied to all training and fine-tuning data
Incident response including model rollback for detected poisoning events

mappings.control_id: Training data must be subject to governance practices — relevant, representative, free of errors
mappings.notes: Data quality controls and provenance requirements preventing poisoning are binding Art. 10 obligations

mappings.control_id: High-risk AI must be resilient to attempts to alter performance through data manipulation
mappings.notes: Technical robustness against poisoning is a binding Art. 15 requirement with conformity assessment evidence

mappings.control_id: Systemic risk GPAI providers must conduct adversarial testing to identify and mitigate systemic risks
mappings.control_name: Art. 55(1)(b) — Systemic risk GPAI adversarial testing
mappings.notes: Poisoning detection adversarial testing is a binding obligation for systemic risk models
Scanning and patching model components and training pipeline dependencies
Security requirements applied to all third-party training data and model sources
Training pipeline designed with integrity controls and supply chain verification
Adversarial testing of model outputs for poisoning indicators before deployment
Model versions, adapters, and datasets managed with integrity and change controls

mappings.control_name: V10.2.1 — Application only uses official repositories
V10.2.2 — Dependency managers check for vulnerabilities

mappings.notes: Training data integrity controls — source allowlisting, anomaly detection, provenance tracking
Continuous monitoring of OT GenAI model outputs — systematic anomalies indicating poisoning detected
Poisoning events treated as Critical security incidents — model suspended, process control fallback activated
Poisoned model availability impact contained — fallback procedure prevents physical process disruption
Pipeline integrity is a core OT security requirement
Data pipeline security in OT risk assessment
Authenticated, integrity-verified data flows across zone boundaries
Training data providers treated as suppliers — provenance, quality, integrity requirements in contracts
Training data protected at rest — integrity verification, source allowlisting, provenance tracking
Monitoring for unauthorised software — model integrity verification at deployment, output anomaly detection
Poisoning incidents analysed — affected training runs, deployed models, downstream impact assessed

mappings.control_id: Poisoning threats documented in GenAI risk assessment — training data, supply chain, model update vectors
Model promotions through change management — integrity verification before production deployment
Training data providers in vendor risk programme — provenance, quality, integrity requirements assessed
Model output anomaly monitoring — poisoning indicators detected before operational impact

mappings.notes: All model promotions tested for unexpected functionality — poisoning detection as Req 6.5 testing requirement
Automated monitoring of GenAI outputs in CDE — systematic anomalies indicating poisoning detected
Poisoning detection in CDE penetration testing programme
Training data providers as TPSPs — data quality and integrity requirements in vendor agreements
All training data sources verified — provenance documented, anomaly detection on data pipelines, integrity checks before ingestion
Model weights verified before deployment — cryptographic signatures, behavioural baseline testing for unexpected outputs
Runtime monitoring for unexpected model behaviour — output distribution drift, sudden accuracy changes, anomalous activation patterns
All external training datasets treated as untrusted supply chain components — provenance, quality review, and integrity verification before use
Design authentication and integrity controls for every pipeline stage
mTLS, input validation, and access controls enforced across pipeline
Infrastructure-as-code with security controls applied at provisioning
Review every pipeline component for authentication and integrity gaps
Regular security updates for all pipeline components

Require mTLS or token auth for all pipeline connections

mappings.control_id: Pipeline service account with access to all stages
mappings.notes: Per-stage credentials with minimum scope

mappings.control_id: Same credential used for multiple pipeline stages
mappings.notes: Separate credentials per stage

mappings.control_name: Protect all code from unauthorised access — data and artefact integrity
mappings.notes: Protect training data, model weights, adapters, and pipeline artefacts from unauthorised modification; enforce write access controls and integrity monitoring

mappings.control_name: Archive and protect software releases — versioned artefact management
mappings.notes: Maintain versioned, integrity-verified snapshots of all training data, model checkpoints, and pipeline artefacts; enable rollback to known-good state

mappings.control_name: Reuse existing well-secured software — dataset and artefact vetting
mappings.notes: Vet all third-party datasets, pre-trained models, and pipeline components for provenance, integrity, and potential poisoning before use
When poisoning is detected, conduct forensic analysis to identify corrupted records, trace to source, and determine blast radius across dependent models

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan — AI data and artefact sources
mappings.notes: Include AI training data, model weights, and pipeline artefacts in supply chain risk management with provenance documentation

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Controls — artefact provenance verification
mappings.notes: Verify integrity and provenance of all AI artefacts using cryptographic signatures and checksums before use in any pipeline

mappings.control_name: Malicious Code Protection — training pipeline integrity
mappings.notes: Extend malicious code protection to training data and model artefacts; detect poisoned data, anomalous patterns, and backdoor indicators

mappings.control_name: Protection of Information at Rest — artefact encryption
mappings.notes: Encrypt all training data, model weights, and pipeline artefacts at rest; enforce key management per FedRAMP requirements

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — training pipeline integrity
mappings.notes: Implement security controls protecting training data and model artefacts from poisoning, tampering, and unauthorised modification
Include data poisoning scenarios in resilience testing; test detection capabilities and recovery procedures for poisoned data and model artefacts

mappings.control_name: Backup Policies — model and data restoration
mappings.notes: Maintain versioned backups of training data and model weights with integrity verification; enable rollback to pre-poisoning states
Conduct post-incident analysis for data poisoning events; trace poisoned content and update protection controls
